# AI Render Runtime

> Rendering engine that converts AI-generated structured data directly into UI components

[English](./README.en.md) | [中文](./README.md)

## Core Value

**AI Native** - Not another React, but a rendering engine designed specifically for AI:

- Input: AI-generated JSON / structured data
- Output: High-performance UI components
- Built-in AI adapters for major LLM providers

## Quick Start

### Method 1: AI-Driven (Core Use Case)

```javascript
import { AIRender } from 'ai-render-runtime';

// Structured data generated by AI
const specs = [
  {
    type: 'card',
    title: 'Sales Report',
    children: [
      { type: 'stats', label: 'Today Revenue', value: '$12,345' },
      { type: 'stats', label: 'Orders', value: '89' }
    ]
  }
];

// Render AI output directly
const app = new AIRender({
  container: '#app',
  initialSpec: specs
});

// Hot update UI when AI returns new data
app.update(newSpecsFromAI);
```

### Method 2: Traditional JSX

```tsx
import { h, render } from 'ai-render-runtime';

const App = () => (
  <div>
    <h1>Hello AI Render</h1>
    <button onClick={() => console.log('clicked')}>Click</button>
  </div>
);

render(<App />, document.getElementById('root'));
```

### Method 3: AI-Enhanced Rendering

```javascript
import { generate } from 'ai-render-runtime';

// Natural language → AI generates UI → Render
const gen = await generate(
  'Create a login form with username, password inputs and submit button',
  '#app',
  'your-api-key'
);
```

## AI-Native Features

### 1. Spec-Driven Rendering

AI returns standardized JSON, automatically mapped to components:

```json
{
  "type": "form",
  "children": [
    { "type": "input", "placeholder": "Username" },
    { "type": "input", "type": "password", "placeholder": "Password" },
    { "type": "button", "label": "Login" }
  ]
}
```

### 2. Built-in Component Registry

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `card` | Card container |
| `form` | Form container |
| `input` | Input field |
| `button` | Button |
| `list` | List |
| `alert` | Alert box |
| `stats` | Statistics card |
| `profile` | User profile card |

### 3. Hot Updates

No page refresh needed when AI returns new data:

```javascript
// Incremental update
app.update(newSpecs);

// Or partial update
app.render(partialSpecs);
```

### 4. AI Adapters

Support for major LLM providers:

```javascript
import { createAIGen } from 'ai-render-runtime';

const gen = createAIGen({
  container: '#app',
  apiKey: 'your-api-key',
  provider: 'minimax'  // or 'openai', 'anthropic'
});

// User interaction → AI understands intent → Generate UI
await gen.generate('Show top 3 selling products this month');
```

## Why AI Render?

Traditional approach:
```
AI returns JSON → Manually write components → Bind data → Test
```

With AI Render:
```
AI returns JSON → AIRender.render() → Done
```

## Architecture

### Reactive Core

- **Signal** - Fine-grained reactivity, precise dependency tracking
- **Proxy Reactivity** - Vue 3-style deep reactivity
- **Computed** - Lazy-evaluated caching

### High-Performance Rendering

- **O(n) Diff** - Key-based difference algorithm
- **PatchFlags** - Fine-grained DOM updates
- **Fiber Scheduler** - Interruptible rendering with priority support

### Modern DX

- JSX support
- Full TypeScript types
- SSR/Hydrate support

## API Overview

### Core

```typescript
// AI-driven rendering
new AIRender({ container, initialSpec })
air.update(specs)

// Traditional rendering
render(vnode, container)

// JSX
jsx(type, props)
```

### Reactivity

```typescript
createSignal(initial)      // Signal
reactive(obj)              // Reactive object
computed(fn)               // Computed value
watch(fn, callback)        // Watch
```

### Components

```typescript
memo(Component)            // Memoize
useMemo(fn, deps)          // Value memoization
useRef(initial?)          // DOM reference
```

### Lifecycle

```typescript
onMounted(fn)             // After mount
onUpdated(fn)             // After update
onUnmounted(fn)           // After unmount
```

### Advanced

```typescript
KeepAlive                  // Component caching
Suspense                   // Async loading
defineAsyncComponent()     // Async component
ErrorBoundary             // Error boundary
```

## Install

```bash
npm install
npm run build
```

## Browser Support

Modern browsers (ES2020+)
